page: Microsoft Azure E32_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 50 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 819 GB, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities alongside weak single-core execution. The single-core performance is constrained, as evidenced by poor ratings in stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and text processing also fall into the bottom 10% of benchmarks. Conversely, multi-threaded operations perform in the middle tier, with an average PassMark CPU score and an average multi-core Geekbench score. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 56.65 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weaker at 24.38 GB/sec.

This server configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio rather than raw single-core compute speed. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is tied to its high resource density, offering a substantial 256 GB memory footprint and 819 GB of local storage on a legacy Intel Xeon platform. It is suitable for relational databases, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise applications where memory capacity is the primary constraint. However, it is less suited for single-threaded processing, high-throughput cryptographic tasks, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32_v3 is an Ev3-family virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with a 2.3 GHz clock speed and 50 MB L3 cache. It includes 819 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor without GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core execution is weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core and memory read benchmarks sit in the average tier. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is configured for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32_v3 is an Ev3-family VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core performance but weak single-core speeds, fitting memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32_v3 is an Ev3-family VM featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density
819 GB of bundled local storage with no GPU acceleration
Average multi-core performance contrasted by weak single-core benchmark results
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
